summary: messages failures --since now skips messages whose timestamp can't be resolved, instead of silently including them.
reason: Same pattern as the earlier compliance_auto_block fix: the guard `!t.IsZero() && t.Before(cutoff)` evaluated false for zero times (when both `timestamp` and `created_at` were NULL or non-RFC3339), so undated messages bypassed the --since window entirely. With --group-by reason, this silently inflated counts with arbitrarily old failures. Fix: invert the polarity to `t.IsZero() || t.Before(cutoff) -> continue`, dropping any row whose timestamp cannot be resolved. Surfaced by Greptile P1 in the PR #417 sixth review pass.
validated_outcome: go build ./... succeeds; go test ./... passes; go vet clean.
